session_hint: the React analytics dashboard scroll freeze investigation
question: What was causing the analytics dashboard to freeze on scroll?
turns: The analytics dashboard in React freezes for 800-1200ms every time a user scrolls quickly. First theory: too many DOM nodes — the table renders all 50,000 rows at once without virtualization.

Added react-window virtualization so only ~30 rows render at a time. DOM node count in DevTools dropped from 150,000 to 400. Scroll still freezes identically. Virtualization wasn't the problem, ruling out DOM node count.

Second theory: CSS box-shadow on table rows forcing GPU compositing thrash. Removed all box-shadow and transition styles from rows. No change in freeze duration. Ruling out CSS rendering as the cause.

Profiled with Chrome DevTools Performance tab. The Main thread shows a 'scroll' event firing 120 times/second, each calling recalculateAggregates() which iterates over all 50,000 raw data points to compute totals. No throttling at all. Adding a 100ms lodash throttle on the scroll handler and memoizing the aggregate result with useMemo eliminated the freeze entirely. Root cause: unthrottled scroll handler recalculating heavy aggregates on every scroll event.
